Code fragment: public final TableField<LinksRecord, Long> LINK_ID = createField(DSL.name("LINK_ID"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-generated primary key (LINK_ID) even though there's already a unique constraint on the LINK column (CONSTRAINT_45), which could serve as a natural primary key since URLs are inherently unique and immutable. Consider using the LINK column as the primary key instead of introducing a surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<LinksListRecord, Long> TRACK_ID = createField(DSL.name("TRACK_ID"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (TRACK_ID) even though a natural unique constraint (UN_TRACK on LINK_ID, TG_CHAT) already exists that could serve as a composite primary key. Consider removing the TRACK_ID column and using the composite key (LINK_ID, TG_CHAT) as the primary key instead.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the CHATS table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., `select(CHATS.TG_CHAT)`.
Using `CHATS.fields()` fetches all columns from the CHATS table, which may include unnecessary data. Explicitly specify only the needed columns, e.g., `select(CHATS.TG_CHAT)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the LINKS table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., `select(LINKS.LINK_ID, LINKS.LINK, LINKS.LAST_CHECKED)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from both joined tables, potentially retrieving unnecessary data. Explicitly list only the needed columns, e.g., `select(LINKS.LINK_ID, LINKS.LINK, LINKS.LAST_CHECKED)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the LINKS table when only specific fields are needed. Specify the required columns explicitly, e.g., `select(LINKS.LINK_ID, LINKS.LINK, LINKS.LAST_CHECKED)`.
